title: Encoder-Only: Bidirectional Representation
concept: Encoder-only models use full self-attention to build bidirectional representations of context, ideal for classification and extraction.

exercises.prompt: What key feature allows encoder-only models to understand the context of a word using both the words before and after it?
exercises.explain: Encoder-only models are known for analyzing the context of text in both directions (left and right), which is called bidirectional representation.

cards.title: Looking Both Ways
cards.body: If you only read a sentence forward, you might miss key clues that come later. What if a model could look at the past, present, and future of a sentence all at once to grasp its true meaning? That's the power of encoder-only architectures!

cards.title: Bidirectional Representation
cards.body: Encoder-only models are neural networks that use bidirectional representation. This means they process text by looking at both the left and right context of every single word at the same time, allowing them to build a deep, complete understanding of the input.

cards.title: The Whole-Page Reader
cards.body: Imagine reading a book where you are allowed to look at both the words before and after a tricky word to guess its meaning. An encoder-only model does exactly this! It doesn't read left-to-right with a blindfold on; it looks at the whole sentence at once to understand each word's full context.

cards.title: Context is Everything
cards.body: Consider the sentence: "The bank of the river was muddy." If a model only reads left-to-right, "bank" could mean a place where money is kept. By looking bidirectionally at "river" (which comes after "bank"), an encoder-only model instantly knows "bank" refers to land near water.

cards.title: Myth: All LLMs Write Text Word-by-Word
cards.body: Many people think all language models work by predicting the next word in a sentence. While decoder models (like ChatGPT) do this, encoder-only models (like BERT) look at all words simultaneously to extract meaning, not to write new sentences.

cards.title: Bidirectional vs. Unidirectional
cards.body: Notice how the encoder-only approach opens up the entire sentence context, while the decoder-only approach restricts the view to only what has been written so far.

right.text: Looks only at past words (left context) to predict the very next word in the sequence.

left.text: Looks at both left and right context (e.g., 'The [blank] of the river') to analyze the whole sentence at once.

exercises.prompt: How do encoder-only models process a sentence to understand its meaning?

options.text: They look at both the left and right context of every word simultaneously.
They only look at words that come before the target word.
They completely ignore the relationship between words.
They only read the very last word of a sentence.

exercises.explain: By using bidirectional self-attention, encoder-only models can look at all words in a sequence simultaneously to build a rich representation of the context.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Encoder-only models are highly suited for classifying text and extracting information because they analyze context bidirectionally.
exercises.explain: Because they look at the whole text at once, encoder-only models excel at understanding and classifying text, rather than generating new text.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the explanation of encoder attention.
exercises.sentence: Encoder-only models use ______ representation to look at both preceding and following words at the same time.
exercises.explain: Encoder-only models use bidirectional attention, meaning they look at words in both directions (left and right) to understand context.

exercises.prompt:
Analyze the two text-processing behaviors:

AI Model A: Reads the entire sentence at once, linking "bank" with "river" which appears later in the sentence.
AI Model B: Reads word-by-word from left to right, hiding all future words while processing the current word.

Which model is encoder-only?

options.text: AI Model A is encoder-only, while AI Model B is decoder-only.
AI Model B is encoder-only, while AI Model A is decoder-only.

exercises.explain: AI Model A is an encoder-only model because it looks at the entire sentence at once (bidirectional). AI Model B is a decoder-only model because it restricts its view to past words only (causal).

exercises.prompt: Why are encoder-only models uniquely suited for tasks like text classification and sentiment analysis?

options.text: They read strictly left-to-right, making them ideal for writing creative essays.
They analyze the whole context bidirectionally, making them excellent at classification tasks.
They hide all words except the first one to prevent the model from getting distracted.
They ignore grammar rules entirely to focus only on punctuation.

exercises.explain: Encoder-only models use bidirectional self-attention to see the entire context (past and future words) at once. This makes them perfect for analyzing text (like sentiment classification or extraction), whereas generating text word-by-word is typically left to decoder-only models.
